input.initialPrompt:
I have an ObservableCollection<Product> with Name and Price properties.
Show me how to display these products in a vertical scrollable list using
CollectionView with a DataTemplate that shows both the name and price.
Use compiled bindings.

data.content:
Here's a MAUI CollectionView with compiled bindings:

**XAML:**
```xml
<CollectionView ItemsSource="{x:Bind ViewModel.Products}">
    <CollectionView.ItemsLayout>
        <LinearItemsLayout Orientation="Vertical" />
    </CollectionView.ItemsLayout>
    <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Product">
            <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                <Label Text="{x:Bind Name}" FontSize="18" FontAttributes="Bold" />
                <Label Text="{x:Bind Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}" FontSize="14" />
            </VerticalStackLayout>
        </DataTemplate>
    </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
</CollectionView>
```

**ViewModel:**
```csharp
public partial class ProductsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Product> products = new();
}
```

**Model:**
```csharp
public class Product
{
    public string Name { get; set; }
    public decimal Price { get; set; }
}
```

Key points:
- `x:DataType="models:Product"` on the `DataTemplate` enables compiled bindings inside it
- `x:Bind` resolves at compile time for better performance and compile-time safety
- `LinearItemsLayout Orientation="Vertical"` makes it scroll vertically (this is also the default)
data.reasoningText: The user wants a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) example showing how to display an ObservableCollection<Product> in a CollectionView with compiled bindings. I'll provide a concise code example.
